clinical_history: This ECG was obtained in a 71-year-old woman, who had just  undergone which of the following emergency operations?
diagnosis: d)  ECG shows a constellation of findings consistent with acute cor pulmonale from massive pulmonary embolus (PE) including: sinus tachycardia; qR in V1; rightward to indeterminate axis; S1, S2, S3 pattern;  incomplete right bundle branch block (RBBB); ST depressions consistent with diffuse subendocardial  ischemia; and right atrial overload (P pulmonale).  Low voltage is a  non-specific post-operative finding here.
